title: tetradenia riparia
intro: Tetradenia riparia, also known as African hyssop, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Madagascar, characterized by its fragrant leaves and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Tetradenia riparia include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In traditional medicine, Tetradenia riparia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling the leaves in water, or as a herbal tea by steeping the leaves in hot water.
